Document: Technical Architecture Overview
=========================================

Section 1: Introduction
-----------------------
This document describes the technical architecture of the distributed processing
system. The system is designed to handle large-scale data processing with high
availability and fault tolerance. Key metrics include throughput of 10,000
events per second and 99.9% uptime SLA.

Section 2: Components
---------------------
The system consists of the following major components:

2.1 API Gateway
The API gateway handles all incoming requests and routes them to the appropriate
microservice. It implements rate limiting, authentication, and request validation.
The gateway uses a round-robin load balancing strategy.

2.2 Processing Engine
The processing engine is the core component that transforms raw data into
actionable insights. It supports both batch and streaming processing modes.
The engine uses a plugin architecture for extensibility.

2.3 Storage Layer
Data is stored in a distributed database with automatic sharding and replication.
The storage layer supports both SQL and NoSQL query patterns.
SECRET_KEY: ARCH_DESIGN_7749

2.4 Message Queue
Asynchronous communication between components uses a message queue system.
Messages are persisted and can be replayed for disaster recovery.

Section 3: Deployment
---------------------
The system is deployed on Kubernetes with the following configuration:
- 3 API gateway replicas
- 5 processing engine workers
- 3-node database cluster
- 2 message queue brokers

Section 4: Monitoring
---------------------
Monitoring is implemented using a combination of:
- Metrics collection (Prometheus-compatible)
- Log aggregation
- Distributed tracing
- Alerting rules for SLA violations

The monitoring dashboard provides real-time visibility into:
- Request latency (p50, p95, p99)
- Error rates by service
- Resource utilization
- Queue depths

Section 5: Security
-------------------
Security measures include:
- TLS 1.3 for all external communication
- mTLS for internal service-to-service communication
- JWT-based authentication
- Role-based access control (RBAC)
- Secrets management via HashiCorp Vault

Section 6: Performance
---------------------
Performance benchmarks show:
- Average response time: 45ms
- P99 response time: 200ms
- Maximum throughput: 15,000 requests/second
- Database query time: 5ms average

Section 7: Disaster Recovery
----------------------------
The disaster recovery plan includes:
- Multi-region deployment
- Automated failover
- Point-in-time recovery for databases
- Regular backup verification

Total word count in this document is approximately 350 words.
The primary author is the Architecture Team.
Last updated: 2024-01-15.

Additional technical notes:
- The system supports horizontal scaling
- Configuration is managed via GitOps
- CI/CD pipeline runs automated tests
- Code coverage requirement is 90%

End of document.
